data.description: Kuwait Electricity Ministry: We completed maintenance of four power transmission lines out of service due to damage caused by falling debris from interceptions.

data.title: Pyramid Ladder Products Introduces Universal Ladder Handle Attachment at National Hardware Show 2026
data.description: At the upcoming National Hardware Show 2026 in Las Vegas, Pyramid Ladder Products will be showcasing a new universal ladder handle attachment at Booth #1764 on March 24. This innovative attachment is designed to create a centered lifting point on ladders, enhancing balance and streamlining transport. The attachment aims to simplify the handling of ladders, making it easier for users. This new product is expected to bring significant convenience and efficiency to the ladder industry.

data.title: Altera and Arm Collaborate to Deliver Efficient, Programmable Solutions for AI Data Centers
data.description: Collaboration between Altera and Arm in San Jose has led to the development of efficient, programmable solutions for AI data centers. This partnership aims to capitalize on the increasing demand for AI capabilities in data centers. The collaboration merges Altera's expertise in FPGA technology with Arm's advanced processors, enhancing the performance and flexibility of AI solutions. With a focus on efficiency, the joint efforts have resulted in solutions that are tailored to meet the demands of AI data centers. This collaboration is expected to significantly impact the market, with Altera's FPGA technology and Arm's processors being at the forefront of this advancement.

data.title: Global Demand for Advanced Adhesive-Dispensing Systems is Growing
data.description: Manufacturers in industries such as automotive, consumer electronics, and medical systems continue to encounter difficulties in adopting advanced technologies like AI, machine learning, and process control. As of March 24, 2026, in Nashville, Tennessee, these sectors are witnessing the impact of artificial intelligence and adaptive process-control technologies on their operations. This signifies a pressing need for these companies to adapt to the evolving technology landscape. The incorporation of such innovations is posing unique challenges, especially with the rapid integration of AI and process-control mechanisms into manufacturing processes.

data.title: FLSmidth & Co. A/S held their Annual General Meeting with notable numbers discussed.
data.description: In the latest statistics released, it has been reported that the total number of COVID-19 cases worldwide has surpassed 100 million, with over 2.1 million deaths recorded. The United States continues to lead in the number of cases, with more than 25 million confirmed infections. In terms of fatalities, Brazil and India are among the worst-hit countries, each reporting over 200,000 deaths. Despite efforts to curb the spread of the virus through vaccination campaigns and public health measures, cases continue to rise in several regions. The global community remains vigilant in the fight against the pandemic, urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines to prevent further transmission of the virus.

data.title: The NRP Group, Denton Housing Authority Break Ground on 297-Unit Affordable Housing Community in Denton
data.description: In Denton, Texas, the NRP Group, a prominent developer, builder, and manager of multifamily housing, in collaboration with the Denton Housing Authority, has revealed the financial closure and commencement of Arbor Ranch. This affordable housing project comprises 297 units and aims to offer cost-effective homes to households earning 30% to 70% of the Area Median Income (AMI). The initiative seeks to enhance the availability of attainable housing options in the region, addressing the pressing need for affordable accommodation.

data.title: Tether announces 'Big Four' firm to conduct first audit of USDT reserves, without revealing the specific company.
data.description: The issuer of the stablecoin did not disclose the specific accounting firm from the group known as the 'Big Four,' which includes Deloitte, PricewaterhouseCoopers, Ernst & Young, and KPMG.

data.title: Bitcoin Crashed 20%+ After Every Bank Of Japan Rate Hike—And Another One May Be Coming
data.description: The Bank of Japan (BOJ) has raised rates four times since March 2024, and each rate hike led to a Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) crash of at least 20%. The most recent increase saw Japanese 10-year yields hit a 27-year high of 2.32% on Monday, raising concerns that another tightening cycle by the BOJ could spark yet another selloff in the crypto market. Following the rate hikes, Bitcoin experienced significant drops. For example, after the March 19, 2024 rate hike from -0.1% to a range of 0-0.1%, Bitcoin fell by approximately 23% in the subsequent weeks. In July 2024, a rate hike to 0.25% caused the yen to appreciate against the dollar, leading to a trillion-dollar global asset selloff. Each BOJ tightening cycle has had a notable impact on Bitcoin prices, underscoring the influence of central bank policies on the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: ViewSonic Launches the ViewBoard IFP41 Series of Interactive Displays with Android™ EDLA Certification
data.description: In Brea, California, ViewSonic has unveiled its new ViewBoard IFP41 Series of Interactive Displays, which are certified with Android™ EDLA. These displays come with built-in access to Google Workspace and Microsoft 365. The release of this series marks an important milestone for ViewSonic as they continue to expand their product line.

data.title: C.H. Robinson named to Fast Company’s annual list of the World’s Most Innovative Companies of 2026
data.description: C.H. Robinson, based in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, has been recognized for its innovation by being included in Fast Company’s prestigious list of the World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2026. This annual list highlights companies that are leading the way in groundbreaking advancements and transformations. With this recognition, C.H. Robinson joins an elite group of innovative companies making significant contributions to their respective industries.

data.title: Unanet and Algorithms Software Expand Partnership to North America, Giving Construction Companies End-to End Capabilities From Opportunity to Delivery
data.description: A recent agreement has been reached between Unanet and Xpedeon America Inc. This collaboration merges top-tier growth and pursuits software with a specialized construction ERP system to offer a unified and connected experience. Unanet, known for its AI-fronted ERP solutions, specializes in software for project-centric enterprises. The partnership aims to combine expertise in this field to provide a seamless experience. The agreement was made public in Dulles, Virginia, on March 24, 2026.

data.title: Operation IFAST Rallies Calix Customers, Equipping Thousands of Technicians To Recognize and Report Human Trafficking
data.description: In San Jose, California, Calix, Inc. (NYSE: CALX) acknowledged the accomplishments of Operation IFAST, a program aimed at combating human trafficking within the telecommunications sector. This initiative provides training to communications service provider (CSP) field technicians on identifying and reporting potential instances of human trafficking during their regular service visits. By partnering with nonprofits and law enforcement agencies, Operation IFAST facilitates collaboration among CSPs to safeguard individuals.

data.title: Linea Energy Closes Project Debt Financing for Watertown Solar in Michigan
data.description: On March 24, 2026, Linea Energy, a renewable energy developer and independent power producer in the United States, sponsored by EnCap Investments L.P., shared that it successfully secured project financing for its Watertown solar project. The specifics of the financial details were not disclosed.
